name: Use of sizeof() on a Pointer Type
description: The code applies sizeof() to a pointer type instead of the underlying data structure, returning the pointer size (typically 4 or 8 bytes) rather than the intended data size. This commonly leads to undersized buffer allocations and subsequent buffer overflow vulnerabilities.
detection_pattern: A TRANSFORM[sizeof] node operates on a pointer variable rather than the pointed-to type, and the result flows into a TRANSFORM[allocation] node (malloc/calloc), producing an undersized buffer without a CONTROL[size_validation] check.
